title: Bathroom Remodel Cost Calculator
subtitle: 7 questions, real numbers from Bozeman bathroom projects. Not national averages — actual Gallatin Valley material and labor costs.

steps.question: What kind of project is this?
steps.subtitle: This sets the baseline for everything else.
steps.why: A full gut remodel means all-new plumbing, waterproofing, and subfloor — a refresh keeps most of that in place. This one question changes the estimate by thousands.

options.label: Full remodel
options.desc: Down to studs — new everything

options.label: Refresh & update
options.desc: New tile, fixtures, finishes — same layout

options.label: Tub → walk-in shower
options.desc: Convert the tub space into a shower

options.label: Fix a specific problem
options.desc: Leak, damage, or one thing to address

steps.question: How big is the bathroom?
steps.subtitle: A rough estimate is totally fine — we'll measure on-site.
steps.why: Labor and materials scale directly with square footage. Tile, waterproofing membrane, and heated floor systems are all priced per square foot, so even a rough size estimate meaningfully affects the range.

options.label: Small
options.desc: Under 40 sq ft (typical half bath or powder room)

options.label: Medium
options.desc: 40–70 sq ft (standard full bathroom)

options.label: Large
options.desc: 70–100 sq ft (primary bathroom)

options.label: Extra large
options.desc: 100+ sq ft (luxury primary suite)

steps.question: What's your shower and tub situation?
steps.subtitle: Select what you want in the finished bathroom.
steps.why: A tub/shower combo is standard plumbing. Separate tub and shower means two drain locations, more tile, and more waterproofing — that's where the cost jumps.

options.label: Shower only
options.desc: Walk-in shower, no tub

options.label: Tub + shower combo
options.desc: Bathtub with a showerhead above

options.label: Separate tub & shower
options.desc: Freestanding tub plus a separate shower

options.label: Tub only
options.desc: Soaking tub, no shower in this room

steps.question: What level of tile work?
steps.subtitle: Tile is often the biggest design choice — and the biggest cost driver.
steps.why: Basic subway tile runs $3–5/sq ft. Natural stone and mosaic work can hit $15–25/sq ft — and takes 2–3× longer to install. Tile choice alone can swing a bathroom by $3,000–8,000.

options.label: Clean & simple
options.desc: Large format subway or single pattern, minimal accent

options.label: Designed & detailed
options.desc: Mixed patterns, accent band or niche feature, quality tile

options.label: Custom & premium
options.desc: Complex patterns, natural stone, mosaic accents, floor-to-ceiling

steps.question: What about fixtures and finishes?
steps.subtitle: Faucets, showerheads, hardware, vanity — the things you touch every day.
steps.why: A standard Moen faucet is $150. A Brizo or Kohler Purist is $400–600. Multiply that across every fixture in the room and the tier choice adds $2,000–8,000 to the project.

options.label: Good quality basics
options.desc: Reliable brands, clean look, great value (Moen, Delta)

options.label: Upgraded selections
options.desc: Designer finishes, rain showerhead, solid hardware (Kohler, Brizo)

options.label: Premium / luxury
options.desc: High-end brands, custom vanity, statement pieces

steps.question: Any special features?
steps.subtitle: Select all that apply. These are common adds that affect the budget.
steps.why: Each of these is a standalone scope item with its own materials and labor. We price these from actual Bozeman project costs — not national averages.

steps.question: When are you hoping to start?
steps.subtitle: No pressure — this helps us plan our schedule.
steps.why: Bozeman remodeling contractors book up — if you're targeting a specific season, starting the conversation early gives you the best shot at getting on the schedule.

options.label: As soon as possible
options.desc: Ready to move forward now

options.label: 1–3 months
options.desc: Planning ahead, want to get quotes

options.label: 3–6 months
options.desc: Still in the thinking stage

options.label: Just exploring
options.desc: Curious about costs, no rush

project_type.full_gut: A full gut means demo to studs — we inspect every layer and rebuild with modern waterproofing.
project_type.refresh: A refresh keeps the layout and plumbing positions but replaces all visible surfaces.
project_type.tub_to_shower: Tub-to-shower conversions involve plumbing relocation and new waterproofing at the drain.
project_type.specific_fix: Targeted repairs address the problem without unnecessary demo — most cost-effective approach.

bathroom_size.small: Small bathrooms are quicker builds but require careful layout planning — every inch matters.
bathroom_size.medium: Standard full bathrooms are our most common project — well-understood scope and timeline.
bathroom_size.large: Primary bathrooms have more tile area, often dual vanities, and larger shower builds.
bathroom_size.xl: Luxury primary suites involve complex tile patterns, freestanding tubs, and custom cabinetry.

shower_tub.shower_only: Walk-in showers are the most popular choice in Bozeman remodels right now.
shower_tub.tub_shower: Tub/shower combos are practical and cost-effective — still the standard in most homes.
shower_tub.both_separate: Separate tub and shower is a premium layout that adds significant plumbing and tile work.
shower_tub.tub_only: Soaking tubs are a design statement — we handle the structural support and drain positioning.

tile_level.basic: Clean subway or large-format tile installs quickly and looks timeless — great value.
tile_level.mid: Mixed patterns and accent features require more layout time but create a custom look.
tile_level.high: Natural stone and mosaic work is labor-intensive — this is where craftsmanship shows most.

fixtures.standard: Moen and Delta deliver reliable quality at a good price point — we install these regularly.
fixtures.upgraded: Kohler and Brizo fixtures are a noticeable step up in feel and finish.
fixtures.premium: High-end fixtures and custom vanities are the details that make a bathroom feel luxurious.

features.heated_floor: Electric radiant heat under tile — a game-changer for Bozeman's cold mornings.
features.niches: Built-in shower niches are tiled into the wall during the waterproofing stage.
features.bench: A tiled shower bench adds comfort and accessibility — built on a waterproofed frame.
features.double_vanity: Double vanities require wider plumbing rough-in and a larger countertop.
features.lighting: Updated lighting and wiring modernizes the space — we coordinate with a licensed electrician.
features.ventilation: Proper ventilation prevents moisture damage — critical in Montana's cold, dry winters.
